requirements.statement: The first crew shall arrive at a powered, provisioned, and fueled Mars base with a demonstrated return path, and no single cargo-ship loss shall defeat the campaign.
requirements.rationale: The program's definition of done for the pre-crew era.
requirements.source: HANDOFF.md section 0; SpaceX-to-Mars deep dive section 6

requirements.statement: Starship shall reach orbit and be recovered.
requirements.rationale: Nothing Mars-bound flies without orbital Starship; nearest gate.
requirements.source: Handmer Feb-2025 "orbit, reuse, refill, chill"; deep dive section 1

requirements.statement: Both Starship stages shall be reused (routine refly).
requirements.source: Handmer Feb-2025; deep dive section 1

requirements.statement: Orbital propellant transfer (refill) shall be demonstrated.
requirements.rationale: A Mars-bound ship launches nearly empty and refills in LEO; undemonstrated mid-2026.
requirements.source: Handmer Feb-2025; GAO/NASA OIG IG-26-004

requirements.statement: Long-duration cryogenic propellant storage (active chill) shall be demonstrated.
requirements.rationale: Cryofuel must survive months in LEO and cruise; boil-off management unproven.
requirements.source: Handmer Feb-2025; deep dive section 1

requirements.statement: The program shall demonstrate survivable EDL of a 100 t-class cargo ship on Mars.
requirements.rationale: Heaviest object ever landed is ~1 t; 20-100x beyond demonstrated.
requirements.source: Deep dive Problem 2 (Braun et al., NTRS 20170008725)

requirements.statement: At least 5 successful cargo landings shall be accumulated before crew commits.
requirements.rationale: Statistical confidence in EDL beyond a single success.

requirements.statement: Follow-on ships shall land within meters of the base (precision landing aids operating).
requirements.source: Manifest section G

requirements.statement: The base shall have continuous, dust-storm-tolerant baseload power.
requirements.rationale: Power is the tall pole; solar collapses in global storms.
requirements.source: Deep dive Problem 3; HANDOFF section 2

requirements.statement: Installed generation shall reach 500 kWe or more in the first window.
requirements.rationale: Pilot ISRU + margin; redundant fleet delivers ~640 kWe.

requirements.statement: Installed generation shall cover the delivered average load at every window.
requirements.rationale: A power-starved plant silently derates ISRU production.

requirements.statement: Installed generation shall reach 1.5 MWe or more before crew arrival.
requirements.rationale: Full-scale ISRU (~850 kW chain) plus habitat and margin.

requirements.statement: The program shall confirm and exploit accessible water ice (prospect, excavate, process).
requirements.source: Deep dive Problem 3; Manifest section C

requirements.statement: The program shall produce and store a full crewed-return propellant load (1,400 t methalox) before crew commits.
requirements.rationale: The single milestone that makes a crewed return credible; the deciding risk.
requirements.source: Deep dive Problem 3; MOXIE reality check

requirements.statement: An integrated pilot chain shall run at 40 kg/hr propellant-equivalent or more in the first window.
requirements.rationale: Proves the full loop (capture-react-electrolyze-liquefy-store) at pilot scale.

requirements.statement: Chain nameplate rate shall reach one return load per synod (>= 112 kg/hr).
requirements.rationale: The rate-matched fuel-factory buy (size_chain design point).

requirements.statement: Cumulative propellant produced and stored shall reach 1,400 t before crew departure.
At least two independent Sabatier chains shall exist on the surface before crew.
requirements.rationale: Plant-level redundancy; a chain fault must not strand the crew.

requirements.statement: An uncrewed Mars-surface-to-Earth return demonstration shall depart before the first crew launches and shall complete before the crew's own return flight, and the crew's carried provisions plus the next synod's resupply shall cover a missed return window if the demonstration fails.
requirements.rationale: L0-MSN-01 promises a demonstrated return path, but producing propellant (L1-PROP-01) is not flying it home. Synodic geometry gives exactly one useful slot: the demonstrator departs Mars ~Jan 2035 (on the doubled 2033 plant's surplus), reports at Earth ~Aug 2035 while the crew is in transit, and the crew's own ~Mar 2037 return flies a profile flown 19 months earlier. The accepted residual is the crew committing (May 2035) three months before the demo reports; if the demo fails and the crew must wait out a window for a fix, the hedge is their own one-synod provisions flown with them plus the next synod's (2037) resupply flight, which arrives before that supply runs out. Nothing consumable pre-lands ahead of the crew (decision 2026-07-21: the only prior pre-land, the survival bridge, was removed once consumables were dropped from the life_support_closed gate).
requirements.source: SpaceX-to-Mars deep dive Problem 1/6; full-profile-uncrewed-first precedent (Crew Dragon Demo-1, Artemis I); return-leg astrodynamics, PROVENANCE section 5a

requirements.statement: An Earth-entry thermal protection system rated for ~11.6 km/s interplanetary-return entry (~8x the heat load of a ~5.7 km/s Mars arrival) shall be qualified.
requirements.rationale: Return entry speed is ~2x Mars arrival and heating scales ~v^3; the return TPS is a distinct qualification item, not the Mars-EDL heat shield reused. This is the sharpest outbound/return asymmetry.
requirements.source: PROVENANCE section 5 (return-leg astrodynamics)

requirements.statement: Mars ascent and trans-Earth injection (~6.5 km/s from the surface, single stage, no Mars-orbit refueling) shall be qualified.
requirements.rationale: Leaving Mars is far cheaper in delta-v than leaving Earth (~5.0 vs 11.2 km/s escape), but the vehicle must lift the entire return load off the surface on ISRU propellant, because there is no orbital tanker at Mars.
requirements.source: PROVENANCE section 5 (return-leg astrodynamics)

requirements.statement: Transfer and loading of ISRU-produced methalox into the return vehicle, and its cryogenic hold through the surface wait, shall be demonstrated; the return flight shall carry propellant samples for Earth-side verification.
requirements.rationale: The propellant the whole architecture depends on must actually be loaded and kept flight-quality on the surface; bringing samples home closes the loop on the deciding risk with the lightest possible cargo.
requirements.source: PROVENANCE section 5; MOXIE reality check

requirements.statement: A full crewed-return propellant load shall be produced and stored before the uncrewed return flight, which consumes a real load.
requirements.rationale: Ties the return-flight demonstration to actual propellant availability; the demonstrator burns ~1,400 t, which the 2033 factory banks by ~late 2034.
requirements.source: Deep dive Problem 3

requirements.statement: A near-closed life-support loop shall be demonstrated on Mars for 1,000 sols before crew.
requirements.rationale: ISS-class ECLSS without spares gives <1% mission reliability; demo runtime is the proof.
requirements.source: Deep dive Problem 5; NTRS ECLSS reliability studies

requirements.statement: The ECLSS demonstration article shall be landed in the first window (demo clock).
requirements.rationale: 1,000 sols of runtime requires delivery no later than 2 windows before crew.

requirements.statement: The first crew shall fly with at least one synod of consumable provisions (food, water, oxygen); nothing consumable need be pre-landed ahead of them.
requirements.rationale: Survival margin travels with the people, not ahead of them. Pre-landing was removed 2026-07-21 (see CONSIDERED and L1-RET-01): per-ship pairing already defeats independent ship loss, and a missed return window is covered by this carried supply plus the next synod's resupply. Three caches (~45 t) is one synod for twelve.

requirements.statement: The life-support demonstration shall run as an integrated article: the ECLSS loop operating inside a landed, pressurized habitat, both on the surface for the full 1,000-sol clock.
requirements.rationale: A closed loop can only be demonstrated against a sealed volume, and the integrated failure modes (interface leaks, condensation on the bladder wall, dust in heat exchangers) are the ones that matter. The habitat itself also needs long-duration demonstration.
requirements.source: Test-as-you-fly precedent: NASA LMLSTP 91-day chamber demo (JSC, 1997); ISS ECLSS chamber testing (MSFC); MELiSSA pilot plant; Lunar Palace 1; BEAM (2016) for inflatable endurance

requirements.statement: Pressurized habitat volume beyond the landed ships shall be ready before crew.
At least 5 habitat modules (rigid or inflatable) shall be on the surface before crew arrival.
A storm shelter and radiation protection shall be in place before crew.
requirements.rationale: Mars surface dose-equivalent is ~230-250 mSv/yr (Guo 2015 ApJ 810:24: 0.7 mSv/day at quality factor Q=3.05; Slaba NASA-TP-2013-217983 Table 1: 155-274 mSv/yr over the solar cycle). Shielding must be IN-SITU regolith, not hauled metal: Slaba shows aluminium INCREASES surface dose (+63% at 100 g/cm2 via secondary neutrons + regolith albedo). That is why `radiation_shelter` is ~1 t of equipment (liners, entrances) gated behind `sintering_rig` - the mass shield is built from regolith on site (~2-3 m / 300-500 g/cm2 for chronic-GCR reduction, 5-8 m for an SPE storm cell), counted as construction workload, not launch mass.
requirements.source: Guo 2015 ApJ 810:24; Slaba/Mertens/Blattnig NASA-TP-2013-217983; deep dive Problem 4 (RAD dose data)

requirements.statement: A surface-to-Earth communications link shall operate from the first window.
The base shall operate autonomously under a 4-22 minute one-way light delay.
Surface mobility for cargo and instruments shall be operational from the first window.
Cargo offload, power distribution, and site logistics shall be operational from the first window.
Every flight window's manifest shall fit within its declared fleet's mass capacity.
Every flight window shall retain at least 10% fleet mass margin for growth and late additions.
requirements.rationale: Concept-stage hardware grows; AIAA practice suggests far more than our 10% contingency. A window at 100% booked has no room for reality.

requirements.statement: No single cargo-ship loss in any window shall cost a schedule-critical capability (power, water, propellant chain, comms, autonomy, infrastructure, mobility, precision landing), accounting for hardware already on the surface.
requirements.rationale: Crew-era capabilities may accept single-hull risk (re-flight margin); schedule-critical ones may not.

requirements.statement: Radiation protection shall be provided by in-situ regolith over the habitats (>= 2-3 m for chronic GCR, a dedicated 5-8 m or water/regolith cell for SPE), not by hauled metal shielding; crew dose shall be held to the exploration budget.
requirements.rationale: Mars surface dose-equivalent ~230-250 mSv/yr (Guo 2015 ApJ 810:24; Slaba NASA-TP-2013-217983). Slaba shows hauled ALUMINIUM increases surface dose (+63% via secondary neutrons), so the mass shield must be in-situ regolith - which is why radiation_shelter depends on sintering_rig.
requirements.source: Guo 2015 ApJ 810:24; Slaba/Mertens/Blattnig NASA-TP-2013-217983

requirements.statement: A launch-grade pad shall be established at >= 1 km debris standoff (sintered in window 0 after water-confirm), the return vehicle shall land on it, and it shall be robotically serviced (TPS survey + tile replacement, engine checkout) and static-fired before the receipt flight.
requirements.rationale: Land-on-pad ground segment: a Starship-class Mars ascent throws ejecta ~1 km (Metzger & Lane 2021, Tier C); the pad is built before its own user so no ~50 m vehicle is moved. Service tower ~150-300 t amortized (Tier C); servicing robotics Tier D.
requirements.source: Metzger & Lane 2021 arXiv 2104.06248; SpaceX catch tower; CONOPS 9/10.3/11

requirements.statement: The designated return candidate shall precision-land on the established launch pad on arrival.
requirements.rationale: The land-on-pad architecture makes precision landing a hard requirement for the return hull - land on a specific pad, not a zone.

requirements.statement: The settlement and its crew rotation shall be sustained safely and indefinitely: keep-alive and rotation logistics, without claiming a self-sufficiency it has not reached.
requirements.rationale: CONOPS-2 (2037+) mission, operations-focused per the program owner: sustain (not necessarily grow or close) the settlement. Its children are Inspection-verified shells that stand OPEN until a city-era model exists - i.e. they are the current design gaps.
requirements.source: CONOPS-2 scoping 2026-07-21

requirements.statement: Crew rotation shall be sustained: at every window, deployed return capacity (berths at 100 pax/ship, banked methalox ~1,200 t/ship, ~50 t consumables/ship) shall meet the reserve policy - full evacuation of the resident population achievable over <= 3 departure windows, rolling-cohort reserve otherwise; the return ground segment (pads x service stands) shall clear scheduled + reserve departures within the ~30-day departure window (pad/stand count a computed output); and no rotating cohort shall be stranded more than one synod past its window.
requirements.rationale: Rotation research 2026-07-21. Mars propellant serves returns only; the full-evac reserve (phased over <=3 windows per owner decision) drives ground-segment sizing; robotic TPS-repair time is the throughput unknown; decouple slow service stands from fast launch mounts.
requirements.source: CONOPS-2 rotation research; NASA DRA 5.0; docs/CONSIDERED.md

requirements.statement: Life support shall be sustained indefinitely without Earth return: O2 recovery >= 75% and whole-loop water >= 95%; each crew-critical function shall carry N+2 redundancy including >= 1 dissimilar path (common-cause coverage); spares shall be Poisson-sized to < 1% stockout per function per synod on demonstrated (not estimated) failure rates; and a standing consumables + critical-spares reserve of >= one missed synod shall be held.
requirements.rationale: Life-support-at-scale research 2026-07-21 (Jones NASA-Ames ICES on ISS MADS): intrinsic MTBF gives whole-system life-support failure ~every 42 days, so sustainment rests on N+2 dissimilar redundancy + demonstrated-MTBF sparing (14/20 ISS ORUs were worse than estimated). Residual resupply is food-dominated (~870 kg/person-yr). Buy-off = repurpose the 1,000-sol ECLSS demonstration.
requirements.source: Jones ICES-2017-84 / ICES-2019-14; NASA BVAD; docs/CONSIDERED.md

requirements.statement: Power shall be sustained at settlement scale: installed generation shall exceed total load with >= 15% reserve margin at every window; installed generation minus the single largest unit shall cover the crew-critical (keep-alive) load, with agricultural grow-lighting shed to a crop keep-alive floor before any crew-critical curtailment; and a funded reactor-replacement cadence (fission units are ~10-year replace assets) shall keep the rolling fleet balance positive, replacements pre-positioned >= 1 synod ahead of end-of-life.
requirements.rationale: Power-sustainment research 2026-07-21 (NASA FSP/Kilopower): the Stirling convertor, not the fuel, is the ~10-yr life-limiter, so end-of-life is REPLACE; a settlement can pass a static capacity test today and brown out later if replacements are not manifested. N+1-on-critical extends the no-single-loss doctrine to the power fleet. Needs a new engine model: asset lifetime/retirement + a critical-vs-sheddable load split.
requirements.source: NASA FSP/Kilopower/KRUSTY; docs/CONSIDERED.md

requirements.statement: Resident health shall be sustained with on-site definitive care (NASA-STD-3001 Level of Care V equivalent: surgery, trauma, imaging, pharmacy trending to on-site production, dental, mental health) and NO reliance on Earth medical evacuation for acute conditions; a surface medical reserve of >= one synod shall be held separately from and shall not draw down the return-propellant bank; no single provider or facility loss shall remove a life-critical capability (>= 2 surgically-competent providers). Reproductive/obstetric capability shall not be established before the ~1,100-resident settlement phase and, when established, shall be a coordinated (managed, gated) capability - free/open reproduction is a post-CONOPS-2 future goal, gated on characterisation of 0.38 g development.
requirements.rationale: Settlement-medical research 2026-07-21: Earth medevac is not a clinical option (synodic window + 210-day transit), so care is treat-in-place and a fueled ambulance-ship reserve buys ~no benefit (hedge is surface inventory). Obstetric policy per the program owner: coordinated only at settlement scale, free reproduction a future goal.
requirements.source: NASA ExMC / NASA-STD-3001; Antarctic/submarine analogs; docs/CONSIDERED.md

requirements.statement: Habitats and infrastructure shall be sustained indefinitely: steady-state atmospheric leakage shall be bounded with a make-up-gas reserve of >= one synod plus one credible breach; each pressure shell shall be certified to factor-of-safety 4.0 on pressure and lifetime (JSC-67721) with the structural shell - not regolith overburden - bearing the pressure load and redundant seals so no single seal failure depressurizes; >= 2-3 m of in-situ regolith radiation cover shall be maintained (sized by radiation, decoupled from dead-weight containment depth); and a recurring maintenance + spares provision of >= 10%/yr of installed sustainment-system dry mass shall be manifested (Earth resupply and/or in-situ manufacture) at < 1%/yr capability-loss probability.
requirements.rationale: Habitat/infra research 2026-07-21: BEAM-class inflatables seal well; JSC-67721 FoS 4.0; because we fly engineered pressure vessels the regolith is sized by radiation (~2-3 m), NOT the ~6.5-19 m dead-weight containment figure (which never applies) - 2-3 m of cover slightly REDUCES net wall stress. The ~10%/yr-of-installed-mass maintenance figure (Handmer) is the dominant cost-to-keep-running, offset only by ISRU-made spares.
requirements.source: NASA JSC-67721; BEAM/TransHab; Handmer make-buy; docs/CONSIDERED.md

requirements.statement: Continuous base-Earth communications shall be maintained through solar conjunction via a Sun-Earth L4/L5 (or heliocentric-drift) relay bridge; the orbital constellation shall be refreshed/augmented on a <= 2-synod cadence before design-life lapse (the window-0 seed expires ~2036-2038); bandwidth shall scale with population; and the segment shall be loss-tolerant (no single-node dependency) since it also carries EDL telemetry and launch weather-watch for rotation launches.
requirements.rationale: Comms-refresh research 2026-07-21 (NASA/AIAA ASCEND): no Mars-local orbit can see around the Sun during conjunction; the single-node fly-once constellation violates the program's own no-single-loss doctrine for a crew-adjacent, schedule-critical asset.
requirements.source: NASA/AIAA ASCEND 2020 NTRS 20205007788; SpaceX Marslink; docs/CONSIDERED.md

requirements.statement: Crew partial-gravity health shall be sustained: adult 0.38 g deconditioning shall be countered by exercise + pharmacologic countermeasures (with an optional short-arm centrifuge reserve), and in-situ human reproduction shall be gated until development in 0.38 g is characterised (linked to L1-MED-01).
requirements.rationale: Gravity-physiology research 2026-07-21: animal centrifuge data (JAXA MARS 0.33 g, MHU lunar) suggest adult 0.38 g is manageable with countermeasures (not a crew gate, do not slip 2035); reproduction/Mars-born-child development is a genuine Tier-D unknown (no mammal has completed a life cycle in reduced g), so it is a timeline gate, not a mass number.
requirements.source: NASA HRP; JAXA MARS/MHU; docs/CONSIDERED.md
